script_text: Reading out loud is like telling a story.
Good readers use their voice to make the words come alive.
Let us learn a few tricks to read out loud like a star!
The ending mark tells your voice what to do.
A period means stop. Your voice comes down. The dog runs.
A question mark means your voice goes up. Is the dog fast?
An exclamation mark means excited! The dog is so fast!
Good readers read at just the right speed.
Not too fast. The words get jumbled.
Not too slow. The words lose their meaning.
Nice and smooth, like talking to a friend.
Pretend you are reading to someone you love. Use your voice!
Now you are ready to practice reading out loud!
